name: Aleister Crowley
biography: Poet, occultist, mountaineer, and polemical writer whose self-authored mythos and ritual corpus define the portal's core subject.

name: Rose Edith Kelly
biography: Crowley's first wife and an important figure in the Cairo Working narrative. Her later life is often flattened by hostile or sensational accounts, so the portal keeps her biography explicit.

name: Victor Neuburg
biography: Poet and magical collaborator who worked with Crowley in the desert Aethyr work and appears throughout the early Thelemic orbit.

name: Leah Hirsig
biography: Crowley's major partner during the Abbey of Thelema years and one of the most important figures in his middle period.

name: Mary Desti
biography: A close companion and collaborator whose presence helps anchor the Paris and New York phases of Crowley's American years.

name: Allan Bennett
biography: An early mentor in ceremonial magic and Buddhism whose influence helped shape Crowley's A.'.A.'. training structure.

name: MacGregor Mathers
biography: Founder of the Golden Dawn order and one of Crowley's major adversaries during the schism years.

name: W. B. Yeats
biography: Poet, Golden Dawn member, and a major literary and occult interlocutor in Crowley's early career and temple disputes.

name: Jane Wolfe
biography: American actress turned disciple who gives the portal a clear line into the Abbey of Thelema and the later preservation of Crowley's work.

name: Jack Parsons
biography: Rocket engineer and occultist whose story links Crowley studies to mid-century American esotericism.

name: Kenneth Grant
biography: A later occult interpreter whose writings extended Crowley's influence into the Typhonian stream and a highly idiosyncratic post-Crowley current.

name: Israel Regardie
biography: Editor, practitioner, and important transmitter of Crowley's ritual and psychological methods to later generations.

name: James Eshelman
biography: A later Thelemic writer and organizer who helps represent the order's modern interpretive afterlife.

name: Richard Kaczynski
biography: Author of one of the most important modern biographies of Crowley and a major archival source for the portal.

name: Tobias Churton
biography: Biographer and historian whose work on Crowley and esoteric history is central to contemporary study.

name: Marco Pasi
biography: Academic historian of Western esotericism whose work helps situate Crowley within broader intellectual history.

name: Martin Starr
biography: Editor and scholar whose work on Crowley's correspondence and historical documents is useful for archival research.

name: Michael Maier
biography: A German physician, counselor to Rudolf II, and a learned alchemist and Rosicrucian apologist.

name: Jacques de Molay
biography: The 23rd and last Grand Master of the Knights Templar and a central martyr figure in later occult myth.

name: Christian Rosenkreuz
biography: Legendary founder of the Rosicrucian Order and a symbolic ancestor for later occult fraternities.

name: Paracelsus
biography: Swiss physician, alchemist, and astrologer of the German Renaissance.

name: Apollonius of Tyana
biography: Greek Neopythagorean philosopher and wonder-worker often invoked as an exemplar of sacred wisdom.
